violations.rule: Back brevity test — parentheticals that add a NEW fact are not OK
violations.detail: The back is "ATP production (cellular energy)" — the parenthetical "(cellular energy)" is a restatement/elaboration adding a second fact, not a standard abbreviation. The SKILL.md explicitly gives "ATP production (cellular energy generation)" as a bad example of this pattern. The back should be just "ATP production" with the explanation as a separate card if needed.
